_doc: Single source of truth for per-model-family prompt wrapping and Ollama generation options. Consumed DIRECTLY by start_local_ai.sh (the watcher) at inference time, and referenced by skill/SKILL.md Step 2 for tuning-file routing. Before 2026-07-01 these values were hand-copied into three places (start_local_ai.sh's if/elif chain, SKILL.md's routing table, protocol/SPEC.md §6.2/§6.3) and drifted out of sync at least once (README.md kept listing granite-code as the active default after granite4.1 became default). Edit THIS FILE when tuning a model family or adding a new one — do not hand-copy these values into the shell script, SKILL.md, or SPEC.md; have them read/reference this file instead. The prose tuning files under skill/models/<name>.md remain the place for qualitative guidance (failure modes, prompt patterns) that a person or Claude reads with judgment — this file is only the small set of deterministic knobs a script needs to parse exactly.

families.notes: Legacy default prior to 2026-06-29. IBM-recommended Question:/Answer: template and generation settings — see https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/watsonx/saas?topic=models-prompting-granite-code
Current default (since 2026-06-29). Follows instructions directly — no Q/A wrapping needed. Ends cleanly, no stop sequence required.

default.notes: Fallback for any model with no dedicated entry above — including models nobody has tuned yet, or a config file that's out of date relative to a newly pulled model. Neutral, model-agnostic settings; also the routing target for Claude's prompt composition (models/_generic.md) when nothing else matches.

_notes.match_semantics: First entry in `families` whose match_prefixes contains a case-insensitive prefix of the active model name wins. If none match, use `default`. `default.match_prefixes` is intentionally empty — it is never matched against, only used as the fallback record.

prompt_wrapping_values.none: Pass the prompt body to Ollama unchanged.
prompt_wrapping_values.ibm_qa:
Wrap as 'Question:
<prompt>

Answer:

' per IBM's Granite Code prompting guidance. Add new wrapping strategies here + implement them in start_local_ai.sh's load_family_config()/wrapping step if a future model family needs something else.

_notes.on_adding_a_model: 1) Pull the model. 2) Add an entry to `families` with match_prefixes, tuning_file, prompt_wrapping, ollama_options. 3) Copy skill/models/_template.md to skill/models/<name>.md and fill it in. 4) Re-run the Setup Wizard's 'Install watcher script' step so the running watcher picks up the updated config. No shell script edits required.
